name: Incentive Audit
summary: Diagnose where political systems quietly reward the wrong behaviour.
whenToUseIt: Use this when a policy, institution, or public system looks reasonable on paper but keeps generating weak results.
steps: Define the official goal of the system.
List the incentives each actor actually faces.
Compare the formal goal with the rewarded behaviour.
Mark where feedback arrives too late or is politically inconvenient.
Suggest one structural change that would reward better behaviour.
example: A reform can claim to be long-term and evidence-based while election cycles, media pressure, and under-resourcing quietly reward short-term symbolism instead.
